commodityFP: Prevent unauthorized use of emergency exit doors while maintaining unimpeded escape capability and alerting to door activation via audible alarm
unspscContext: No 8-digit UNSPSC commodity precisely captures this product. Product sits at intersection of UNSPSC 46171500 (Locks and security hardware) and 46171600 (Alarm systems). Custom product group 'Emergency Exit Door Alarm Guards' selected.
fpExtension: Monitor distributed safety-critical hardware wirelessly, detecting activation events and battery status without mains wiring at the device point, and aggregating status to a centralized building management system
extensionDomains: Fire suppression hardware monitoring; Emergency lighting status aggregation; Industrial safety valve surveillance; Elevator escape hatch monitoring; Any fleet of distributed passive safety devices

candidates.title: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions

archDistanceData.title: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions
Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services

candidateDetails.title: General Medical and Surgical Hospitals
candidateDetails.job: Secure restricted-zone emergency exits against unauthorized patient elopement or unauthorized access while maintaining fire-code-compliant escape routes
candidateDetails.whyNeeded: Hospitals must balance patient containment (psychiatric, dementia, pediatric) with fire safety evacuation compliance. Current solutions range from manual door checks to expensive wired electronic systems. Battery-powered wireless monitoring enables retrofit across legacy hospital wings.
candidateDetails.alternatives: Wired electronic access control (dormakaba, ASSA ABLOY), nurse-station door alarms, manual patrol rounds, CCTV-based monitoring

candidateDetails.title: Elementary and Secondary Schools
candidateDetails.job: Monitor all emergency exit doors for unauthorized opening during school hours while maintaining instant evacuation capability during emergencies
candidateDetails.whyNeeded: Schools operate under extreme dual pressure: fire marshals require unimpeded exits; security protocols require controlled access. Budget-constrained school districts need retrofit-friendly, low-maintenance solutions.
candidateDetails.alternatives: Mechanical-only door alarms, wired alarm panels, CCTV at exits, security personnel, door prop alarms

candidateDetails.title: Hotels (except Casino Hotels) and Motels
candidateDetails.job: Prevent guest misuse of emergency stairwell and fire exit doors while maintaining fire evacuation compliance across all floors and wings
candidateDetails.whyNeeded: Large hotels may have 50-100+ emergency exit points across floors. Manual patrol is impractical. Wireless, battery-operated monitoring with centralized dashboards enables portfolio-wide visibility for hotel management companies.
candidateDetails.alternatives: Stairwell re-entry locks, mechanical door alarms, wired alarm systems integrated with property management, security guard patrols

candidateDetails.title: Lessors of Nonresidential Buildings
candidateDetails.job: Maintain fire-code-compliant emergency exits across multi-tenant commercial buildings while monitoring for unauthorized use and generating audit-ready compliance documentation
candidateDetails.whyNeeded: Office building portfolios may span hundreds of emergency exit doors across multiple properties. Landlords need cost-effective, scalable monitoring that works in existing buildings without costly wiring retrofits.
candidateDetails.alternatives: Basic mechanical door alarms, wired fire alarm panel integration, security guard patrols, tenant-managed access control

candidateDetails.title: Continuing Care Retirement Communities
candidateDetails.job: Prevent cognitively impaired residents from eloping through emergency exits while maintaining instant evacuation capability for staff-assisted emergency egress
candidateDetails.whyNeeded: Elopement prevention is a major liability and regulatory concern in senior care. Current wired wander-management systems are expensive and complex. A simpler, retrofit-ready alarm + wireless monitoring system provides a cost-effective layer of protection.
candidateDetails.alternatives: RFID/BLE wander-management systems (Stanley Healthcare, CenTrak), delayed-egress electromagnetic locks, coded keypad exits, staff stationed at exits

candidateDetails.title: Facilities Support Services
candidateDetails.job: Efficiently manage fire safety compliance and emergency exit door monitoring across a portfolio of client buildings with minimal on-site labor
candidateDetails.whyNeeded: FM companies need standardized, low-maintenance solutions deployable across diverse building types. Wireless IoT monitoring with centralized dashboards aligns with their service model: remote monitoring reduces site visits.
candidateDetails.alternatives: Manual inspection rounds, wired alarm panels per building, paper-based compliance logs, third-party security monitoring services

candidateDetails.title: General Warehousing and Storage
candidateDetails.job: Prevent unauthorized propping or misuse of emergency exit doors in warehouse facilities while maintaining fire evacuation compliance for large-footprint buildings
candidateDetails.whyNeeded: Warehouses have expansive layouts with exits far from management offices. Manual monitoring is impractical across 3-shift operations. Battery-powered wireless monitoring is ideal because warehouse emergency exits are far from electrical infrastructure.
candidateDetails.alternatives: Door prop alarms, mechanical door alarms, wired alarm systems, security cameras at exits, guard patrols

candidateDetails.title: Colleges, Universities, and Professional Schools
candidateDetails.job: Monitor emergency exit doors across distributed campus buildings for unauthorized access or exit while maintaining fire evacuation compliance
candidateDetails.whyNeeded: Universities have distributed, aging building stock where wired alarm systems are expensive to retrofit. Wireless, battery-operated monitoring allows incremental deployment building-by-building.
candidateDetails.alternatives: Campus access card systems, wired door alarms, CCTV monitoring, campus police patrols, mechanical door alarms

candidateDetails.title: Museums, Historical Sites, and Similar Institutions
candidateDetails.job: Protect exhibition and storage areas by monitoring emergency exits for unauthorized use while maintaining fire evacuation compliance in heritage-listed buildings
candidateDetails.whyNeeded: Heritage building constraints make wired security systems difficult or impossible to install. Retrofit-ready, non-invasive wireless monitoring solves the heritage-vs-security tension.
candidateDetails.alternatives: Guard patrols, CCTV, basic mechanical door alarms, gallery attendants, wired intrusion detection systems

candidateDetails.title: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services
candidateDetails.job: Log and monitor every emergency exit door event across data center facilities for security compliance auditing while maintaining fire evacuation readiness
candidateDetails.whyNeeded: Data centers require documented door-event logs for compliance certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27001). Wireless monitoring supplements wired access control on secondary exits and provides redundant monitoring.
candidateDetails.alternatives: Enterprise access control systems (Honeywell, Lenel), biometric entry systems, mantrap configurations, CCTV with analytics
